_meta.scope: P0.3 from reports/SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP.md — kernel field masking coverage

title: P0.3 — extend kernel field masking to flagged sensitive fields + platform-admin unmask escape
business_reason: The masking ENGINE is already wired into the kernel (list/get/create/update all mask), but the rule set (config/masking.json) was sparse — many sensitive fields (coordinator PII, private file refs, secrets, recipient contacts, provider refs, raw OMR payloads) were returned in clear to non-privileged actors. This extends coverage and adds the standard platform-admin escape.
brain_skills: Skills: 08 Security (least privilege / PII), 11 Data Classification. Brain: sensitive fields must be masked by default, unmasked only for those who need them.

param_14.question: Mask the sensitive fields named in data_classification across modules.
param_14.answer: Added 10 rules to config/masking.json (coordinator_email/phone/mobile, document_file, setting_value, channel_address, recipient_email/phone, provider_reference, response_payload) with appropriate masks (partial_email / last_4_only / hidden / never_return_raw) and module-appropriate unmask_roles. Added an ALWAYS_UNMASK escape for super_admin/system_admin (top roles see everything; keeps admin views + e2e correct). The kernel already applies maskRecord on every read path.
param_14.answer_source: spec/modules/<m>/data_classification.json (sensitive/restricted classes) + the existing masking engine
param_14.security_implications: Non-privileged staff and school actors no longer see these PII/secret/raw fields; admins and the relevant module roles do. Scoped, controlled extension (not a blind auto-sweep) to avoid over-masking.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: A full data_classification.json -> masking.json GENERATOR (with per-field role mapping from access_matrix) is a follow-up (P0.3b) — needs careful e2e re-validation; verification_code masking needs own-record logic (school sees its own) so deferred.
param_14.assumptions: field_pattern substring matching is acceptable; admin escape is correct (top roles hold all grants).

principles_pass.P0.6: config-driven rules over the shared engine; all modules benefit
principles_pass.P3.8: least privilege with explicit unmask roles

functional: non-privileged staff: coordinator_email partial, phone last4, document_file/setting_value null, provider_reference last4; super_admin sees all; operations_head sees coordinator PII but not settings secrets (unit masking_extended 3/3)
regression: full e2e green; chain3 surfaced a STALE fixture (batch already locked from a prior run -> new P0.1 guard correctly blocks re-lock) -> made chain3 order-independent (assert persisted effect)
security_owasp: A01/A09: PII/secrets/raw payloads masked from non-privileged actors at the kernel

verification: tsc clean; unit 162/162 (masking_extended 3/3); full e2e green (chain3 fixture fixed; 02 cold-start flake confirmed passing alone).

known_followups.P0.3b: generate masking.json from data_classification.json + access_matrix; own-record unmask for verification_code so a school sees its own certificate code.
